perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to use the Confidential Information exclusively for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Party receiving Confidential Information (“Receiving Party”) shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner or disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent of the Party making the disclosure (“Disclosing Party”).
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to use the Confidential Information exclusively for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction, unless otherwise requested by the other party. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Party receiving Confidential Information (“Receiving Party”) shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner or disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent of the Party making the disclosure (“Disclosing Party”).
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise requested by the other party", the Receiving Party might use or disclose Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's consent which is contradicted by the rest of the paragraph: "the Party receiving Confidential Information (“Receiving Party”) shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner or disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent of the Party making the disclosure (“Disclosing Party”).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to use the Confidential Information exclusively for the purpose of the Proposed Transaction, unless otherwise requested by the other party. Except as expressly provided in this Agreement, the Party receiving Confidential Information (“Receiving Party”) shall not use the Confidential Information in any manner or disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent of the Party making the disclosure (“Disclosing Party”).
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds "unless otherwise requested by the other party" to the original agreement about confidential information use. This addition directly contradicts the subsequent clause in the same paragraph, which states the Receiving Party cannot use or disclose Confidential Information without the Disclosing Party's prior written consent. The contradicted text is the same location as the changed text, but the modification introduces conflicting instructions regarding confidential information handling.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it will use diligent efforts to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including implementing equivalent security measures and degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own proprietary or confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts, but not necessarily diligent efforts, to protect the secrecy and confidentiality of and avoid disclosure of the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, including implementing equivalent security measures and degree of care that the Receiving Party uses to protect its own proprietary or confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: The change from "diligent efforts" to "commercially reasonable efforts" lowers the standard of care. This creates a contradiction with Section 13, which allows the owner of Confidential Information to "seek injunctive relief for any violation of this Agreement," as a lower standard of care might make it more difficult to prove a violation worthy of injunctive relief. Specifically, what constitutes a 'violation' becomes unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief. Each Party acknowledges and agrees that disclosure or use of Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the owner thereof, for which monetary damages may be difficult to ascertain or be an inadequate remedy. Therefore, each Party agrees that the owner of Confidential Information may seek, in addition to its other rights and remedies, to seek injunctive relief for any violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'diligent efforts' to 'commercially reasonable efforts' weakens the standard of care required of the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which allows the owner of Confidential Information to seek injunctive relief for 'any violation of this Agreement.' The lower standard of care introduced by the change makes the definition of 'violation' unclear. A commercially reasonable effort might still result in a disclosure of confidential information, but it might not be considered a violation worthy of injunctive relief, creating a conflict with the original intent and enforceability of the injunctive relief clause.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party will have no rights, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information except as expressly provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, unless the Receiving party improves upon the information. The Receiving Party will have no rights, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information except as expressly provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "unless the Receiving party improves upon the information" contradicts the first sentence of this section that "All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party". If the receiving party is able to 'improve upon the information' and as a result, acquire property rights, the Confidential Information does not remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party, unless the Receiving party improves upon the information. The Receiving Party will have no rights, by license or otherwise, to use or disseminate the Confidential Information except as expressly provided in this Agreement. No patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right is licensed, granted or otherwise conveyed by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the phrase "unless the Receiving party improves upon the information" to the statement that "All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party." This directly contradicts the original statement by allowing the Receiving Party to potentially gain ownership rights through improvements, which is incompatible with the Disclosing Party maintaining exclusive ownership. This contradiction is also reflected in the 'Contradicted Text' which is identical to the 'Changed Text', highlighting that the change itself introduces the contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information), unless the receiving party reasonably believes it needs to keep the information for ongoing legal or business matters.
perturbation.explanation: The addition "unless the receiving party reasonably believes it needs to keep the information for ongoing legal or business matters" creates a loophole that contradicts the initial requirement for prompt return or destruction of confidential information. This introduces uncertainty about when the Receiving Party is actually obligated to return or destroy the information, potentially hindering the Disclosing Party's ability to protect its confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days of receiving notice from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy (and verify in writing its destruction) all material embodying Confidential Information (in any form and including, without limitation, all summaries, copies and excerpts of Confidential Information and all electronic media or records containing or derived from Confidential Information), unless the receiving party reasonably believes it needs to keep the information for ongoing legal or business matters.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('unless the receiving party reasonably believes it needs to keep the information for ongoing legal or business matters') to the original requirement for prompt return or destruction of confidential information. This directly contradicts the initial obligation, creating uncertainty about when the Receiving Party is actually obligated to return or destroy the information. Since the contradicted text also contains the same contradiction and is in the same location, it is valid to assign YES, as it creates an exception, or "loophole" to the action of returning or destroying data in 10 days, according to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction Definition
